perturbation.original_text: Prospect and Prospect’s Broker acknowledge that all information and materials provided by Listing Broker regarding the above-referenced Property is conf idential and may not be used for any purpose other than evaluation. Prospect’s and Prospect’s Broker’s dissemination of any inform ation and materials provided by Listing Broker will be limited to attorneys, accountants, banking represen tatives, and business advisors directly involved with the above-referenced Property. In the event the transaction is not successful, Prospect and Prospect’s Broker will immediately return to Listing Broker any information and materials provided by Listing Broker .
perturbation.changed_text: Prospect and Prospect’s Broker acknowledge that all information and materials provided by Listing Broker regarding the above-referenced Property is confidential. In the event the transaction is not successful, Prospect and Prospect’s Broker will return to Listing Broker any information and materials provided by Listing Broker .
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause restricting dissemination to specific professionals, the modified text creates a contradiction. The agreement implies confidentiality, but no longer restricts who can access the confidential information, thus weakening the core purpose of the agreement. Specifically, it now contradicts the intention of confidentiality, since there is no limitation on disclosure anymore. By removing the term, the confidentiality no longer covers and attorney, accountants, banking representatives, and business advisors.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidentiality. Prospect and Prospect’s Broker acknowledge that all information and materials provided by Listing Broker regarding the above-referenced Property is confidential. In the event the transaction is not successful, Prospect and Prospect’s Broker will return to Listing Broker any information and materials provided by Listing Broker .
perturbation.justification: The original text restricted dissemination of confidential information to specific professionals. The changed text removes this restriction, contradicting the intention of confidentiality expressed in both the changed text and the contradicted text by allowing disclosure to anyone. By removing the term, the confidentiality no longer covers attorneys, accountants, banking representatives, and business advisors which causes a clear and direct conflict and creates a contradiction. This aligns with the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Prospect and Prospect’s Broker agree not to contact the Property owner, landlord, tenants, employees, or customers except through Listing Broker . Prospect and Prospect’s Broker further agree not to circumvent or interfere with Listing Broker’s contract with owner/landlord in any way.
perturbation.changed_text: Prospect and Prospect’s Broker agree not to contact the Property owner, landlord, tenants, employees, or customers except through Listing Broker .
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'Prospect and Prospect’s Broker further agree not to circumvent or interfere with Listing Broker’s contract with owner/landlord in any way.' allows the Prospect and Prospect's broker to interfere with the Listing Broker's contract. This directly contradicts the implied agreement to respect the listing broker's position and undermines the fair dealing expected in such agreements. The omission impacts the integrity of the relationship established by the agreement, because the Listing Broker may lose the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Circumvention. Prospect and Prospect’s Broker agree not to contact the Property owner, landlord, tenants, employees, or customers except through Listing Broker .
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly prohibited the Prospect and Prospect's Broker from circumventing or interfering with the Listing Broker's contract. The changed text removes this explicit prohibition. The contradicted text, which is identical to the part of the original text that remains in the changed text, states that the Prospect and Prospect's Broker agree not to contact the property owner except through the Listing Broker. The omission of the non-circumvention clause allows the Prospect and Prospect's broker to interfere with the Listing Broker's contract, creating an in-text contradiction because it undermines the integrity of the agreement to respect the Listing Broker's position, which the contradicted text relies upon.

perturbation.original_text: No representation is made by Listing Broker as to the accuracy of the information and materials provided. Prospect and Prospect’s Broker agree to thoroughly review and in dependently verify the information and materials provided. Listing Broker advises Prospect and Prospect’s Broker to consult appropriate professionals for legal, tax, environmental, and other specialized advice concerning matters affecting th e Property and the transaction contemplated.
perturbation.changed_text: No representation is made by Listing Broker as to the accuracy of the information and materials provided. Listing Broker advises Prospect and Prospect’s Broker to consult appropriate professionals for legal, tax, environmental, and other specialized advice concerning matters affecting th e Property and the transaction contemplated.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'Prospect and Prospect’s Broker agree to thoroughly review and in dependently verify the information and materials provided.', the Listing Broker takes more responsibility about the accuracy of the data, even when is not representing as accurate. This creates contradiction in the text itself. The omission is the key reason for this conflict.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Verification of Data. No representation is made by Listing Broker as to the accuracy of the information and materials provided. Listing Broker advises Prospect and Prospect’s Broker to consult appropriate professionals for legal, tax, environmental, and other specialized advice concerning matters affecting th e Property and the transaction contemplated.
perturbation.justification: The original text included a clause stating that the 'Prospect and Prospect’s Broker agree to thoroughly review and independently verify the information and materials provided.' This placed the onus of verification on the prospect. The changed text omits this clause, implying that the Listing Broker holds more responsibility for the accuracy of the information, even while stating 'No representation is made by Listing Broker as to the accuracy of the information'. This directly contradicts the 'Verification of Data' section, which states the same as the original text.
